behaviors.description: The scoring model assigns the correct risk score when a transaction exceeds the configured amount threshold for its merchant category.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local rule evaluation: merchant-category threshold lookup plus score computation. No OS/network/subprocess/LLM dependency is required to catch incorrect threshold comparison or score math.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the scoring model with an in-memory config containing category-specific amount thresholds. Evaluate a transaction just above the category threshold and assert the exact numeric score increment and final score. Add boundary assertions at threshold and just below threshold to catch comparison-operator errors.

behaviors.description: The risk model identifies a fraudulent transaction pattern (e.g., rapid small purchases from different geographies) even when no single rule threshold is exceeded.
behaviors.justification: This asserts model classification quality (fraud-pattern detection) rather than only wiring. Per the rubric, correctness claims about model classify/score behavior require real model execution; mocks could always pass and hide true misclassification.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real risk model on a labeled fraud-pattern fixture set where each transaction stays under rule thresholds but the sequence indicates fraud (rapid low-value cross-geo activity). Assert the model outputs fraud probability above the review threshold for positive cases and below threshold for matched benign controls. Repeat across multiple runs/seeds if applicable and enforce pass-rate criteria.

behaviors.description: The velocity check in the scoring model correctly counts the number of transactions within the configured time window per user.
behaviors.justification: Real failures commonly occur at module boundaries: transaction store query/filtering, timestamp handling, and clock/window logic. A pure in-process mock can miss off-by-one and persistence/query semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Use a local test DB/repository and real velocity-check component with an injected test clock. Seed transactions for one user around window boundaries (inside, exactly at start/end, just outside). Execute the scoring path and assert the velocity count used in scoring matches expected inclusive/exclusive window semantics.

behaviors.description: A transaction flagged by the risk model but not by the scoring model is still held for review — the models operate as an OR gate, not AND.
behaviors.justification: The core risk is orchestration/wiring between two model outputs and hold-decision logic. Unit-testing a boolean expression alone can miss contract mismatches between modules (field names, null/default handling, adapter behavior).
behaviors.test_description: Wire the transaction evaluation service with real decision/orchestration modules and deterministic stubs for model outputs (scoring=false, risk=true). Execute evaluation and assert review_hold=true and persisted decision reason includes risk-model trigger. Add complementary case (true,false) and (false,false) to confirm OR semantics.

behaviors.description: The combined output includes both the numeric risk score and the risk model's fraud probability, so the reviewer can see which model flagged the transaction and why.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is composition/serialization of multi-model outputs. You need real assembler/DTO/schema mapping across modules; unit tests of each model output independently can miss dropped/renamed fields in combined payload.
behaviors.test_description: Run the evaluation pipeline through the real result-aggregation and serialization layer (in-process), with deterministic model outputs. Assert the emitted review payload contains numeric score, fraud probability, per-model flag status, and explanation fields with correct schema keys/types.
